title: quercus stellipila
intro: Quercus stellipila, also known as the star-shaped oak, is a species of tree native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The bark and leaves of Quercus stellipila have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing relief from digestive problems. Quercus stellipila is typically prepared as a decoction, made by steeping the bark and leaves in hot water, and can also be used as a tincture or powder in herbal formulations.
